verdict.summary: HBL Engineering scores 46/100 with strong trailing fundamentals (ROE 45%, ROCE 58%, near-zero debt) but fails Gate 1 Trend Template on 6 of 8 criteria — price is 34% below its 52-week high, below its declining 50/150/200 DMAs, RS percentile is a weak 23, and the most recent quarter shows EPS growth decelerating sharply from triple digits to 79%. This is a broken/corrective stock, not a Stage 2 leader, and does not qualify as a Minervini candidate regardless of its cheap-looking valuation or historical growth quality.
strengths: Exceptional ROE (45.3%) and ROCE (58.4%) with minimal debt (D/E 0.03) and 74x interest coverage
3-year EPS CAGR of 102% and sales CAGR of 34% show a genuine multi-year growth run
Zero share dilution over 2 years and stable promoter holding (~59%) signal no capital-structure red flags
weaknesses: Fails 6 of 8 Trend Template criteria: price below falling 50/150/200 DMAs, price 34% off 52-week high, RS percentile only 23
Latest quarter EPS growth decelerated to 79% YoY from 343% and 242% the prior two quarters — clear deceleration trend, not acceleration
Stock is deep in a 45% drawdown base spanning 34 weeks, well outside any buyable structure
Institutional holding is thin (6.7%) and choppy quarter to quarter, with no marquee sponsor identified
Several fundamental fields (pledge %, FCF trend, group RS rank) remain unverified, capping related rubric points at zero
verdict.biggest_risk: EPS_DECELERATION — the sequential slowdown in YoY EPS growth (343% → 242% → 79%) suggests the earnings surge that drove the prior uptrend is fading, which aligns with and likely explains the ongoing price breakdown below all key moving averages.

verdict.summary: GRSE scores 14/100 and fails the Trend Template (c2, c5, c8), so per Prime Directive 4 this is a hard REJECT/AVOID regardless of the base structure or promoter stability observed. RS percentile of 27 and price below both the 50 DMA and prior pivot confirm the stock is not in a confirmed Stage 2 uptrend. Nearly all fundamental inputs (earnings, revenue, profitability, balance sheet ratios) are unverified and correctly scored zero — there is no fundamental case to evaluate until data is sourced. This is not an actionable setup in any capacity.
strengths: Promoter holding stable at 74.5% across all 4 quarters (E4)
Institutional holding ticked up last quarter (E1)
Volume dry-up in final contraction (-57% vs 50d avg) is technically constructive (G5)
Base depth moderate at 24.2% (G3), not yet a red-flag deep base
weaknesses: Fails Trend Template on c2 (150 DMA not above 200 DMA), c5 (price below 50 DMA), and c8 (RS percentile 27, far below the 70 threshold)
50 DMA is falling, not rising — short-term trend is down
Price is 10.8% below the pivot and 21.6% below the 52-week high
Entire earnings block (25 pts) scored zero due to unverified EPS/revenue data — no visibility into fundamental momentum
